dialogos.texto_en: Uesugi-kun, are you starting to feel hungry?
...What kind of response\nare you expecting from me here?
D-don't overthink it! I was just suggesting it\nsince we've been walking around quite a bit,\nso it's only natural to feel hungry, right?
Haha, I was just kidding.\nActually, I'm feeling a bit hungry myself.
Ah, but just for a light snack though, okay?\nI don't want to pig out or anything.
Why are you emphasizing THAT?!
Come on...\nI just thought there's a good place nearby\nand wanted to suggest it.
A good place?
A specialty store for pork and egg rice balls!
Ah, that's the Okinawan soul food, right?
Yes, I've been wanting to try it!
...So you were heading towards the shop\nbefore even asking me about my hunger level?
T-that's not the case, okay?
Your eyes are wandering.\nAnyway, never mind.
This menu...has quite a few options.
The side menu looks extensive too.\nThe goya tempura thing looks delicious.
I'm having a hard time deciding,\nbut, hmm%p-2;――
In the end, we both went with the plain pork and egg rice ball.
Indeed, starting with the classics is always a safe bet!
It'll all be delicious.\nLet's go with the classic, then.
What's with that look?
I agree that they'd all taste great,\nbut you can't even distinguish flavors\nto begin with...
I'm not that bad!\nI can at least tell the difference\nin rice ball fillings!
Jeez...\nlet's just eat already.
Yes, I agree.\nIt's the most delicious\nwhen it's freshly made and still warm.
Alright then, let's eat!
It wouldn't hurt to try something adventurous.\nWe can only eat these rice balls in Okinawa, after all.
W-why would you say something like that?!
Huh? Did I say something that awful?
We've already thought about it\nand decided on the classic,\nso please don't confuse me!
Is that MY fault?!
So, let's just come back later and get another one%p-2;――\n%p;W-well, I wonder if it's too much to get two.
The classic is a classic because it's delicious.\nLet's be satisfied with this.
Y-yes, you're right!\nLet's go with a guaranteed flavor!
...Alright, let's eat up.
Yes, I agree.\nIt's the most delicious\nwhen it's freshly made and still warm.
Alright then, let's eat!
Ah, well, classics are classics for a reason.\nIt's because you only need to know the basics!
You used to say that a lot during study sessions.
You guys were all over the place with your basics,\nbut you were trying to move forward\njust for the sake of scores.
Ooohh...\nI'm so embarrassed...
Well, you did your best in that situation%p-2;――
Oops, sorry. I didn't mean to lecture you.\nOh, no, am I still in tutor mode?
We were teacher and student\nfrom when we met until the end of exams, after all.
But now, we're not teacher and student anymore...
O-oh my!\nI feel embarrassed thinking about it again!
...Alright, let's eat up.
Yes, I agree.\nIt's the most delicious\nwhen it's freshly made and still warm.
Alright then, let's eat!
Nom...
Oh, this is...
So delicious!
Ah, the saltiness is just perfect.
The pork already has salt seasoning.\nI think that's the taste%p-2;――
Just that alone, and it's this delicious.\nI wonder if it's the perfect combination with the egg.
In Okinawa, they eat this for breakfast, right?\nIt's definitely enough\nto keep you energized for the whole day.
It's a strong flavor, but at the same time,\nthere's something gentle about it%p-2;――
Mmm, I can't stop!\nI could probably eat one more!
(As expected of Itsuki,\n she looks so happy when she eats.\n Just watching her makes me happy too.)
What are you staring at me like that for?
When I watch you eat,\nit makes the food taste even better.
...Are you complimenting me?
That's the plan.
Hmm...\nI have mixed feelings about that.
